269120.explanation: D66 wants to investigate the possibility of a direct bus connection from Zwolle to Ter Apel. We see moving the bus stop from the center to Emmen South primarily as shifting the problem within Emmen, which we therefore find undesirable.

269121.explanation: D66 wants to fully invest in developing the Greenwise Campus because that is investing in Emmen’s future. It is not only intended for organizing education but also as an innovation center where entrepreneurs can experiment with new technologies.

269123.explanation: It is important that residents can always receive the right care. D66 wants to prioritize tackling so-called care cowboys. By working with fewer different care providers, it becomes easier to monitor them and safeguard care quality.

269124.explanation: D66 wants to fully commit to realizing the Nedersaksenlijn railway. It is important the municipality thinks smartly about building plans, integration, housing construction, and connecting education and entrepreneurs. Expropriation could be a last resort but preferably not.

269125.explanation: D66 wants to fully commit to municipal heating networks. We want to use residual heat generated, for example, at the GETEC park. This is not only a sustainable solution but could also significantly lower energy bills for all participating residents.

269126.explanation: D66 wants to make the municipality smoke-free as soon as possible. Schoolyards are already smoke-free by law. In addition, we want policies developed to make sports clubs, playgrounds, and terraces smoke-free as well.

269127.explanation: In all villages and neighborhoods, residents must be able to walk safely on the streets without worrying about antisocial road users. However, D66 does not believe that installing speed bumps everywhere always contributes to this. There are other adjustments possible that can be more effective.

269129.explanation: Municipal civil servants deserve appreciation. The municipality's range of tasks continues to expand. Moreover, the work is becoming increasingly complex. Therefore, D66 absolutely does not want to cut spending on civil servants but rather to structurally invest in well-trained staff and competitive salaries.

269130.explanation: D66 believes the polluter should pay. For that reason, we have been and remain supporters of pay-as-you-throw (diftar). A system of post-sorting waste is extremely expensive and deprives residents of the opportunity to manage their own waste and thus reduce waste disposal charges.

269131.explanation: D66 finds it important that everyone in Emmen feels welcome and that diversity is celebrated. Various organizations and institutions such as Liefdes Circus, the Rainbow Festival, Facet, and Stichting LHBT Emmen play an important role in this. They deserve the municipality's support.

269132.explanation: D66 wants that for short distances it is no longer necessary to use the car. Therefore, we want to organize the municipality so that pedestrians and cyclists have as much priority as possible over cars.

269133.explanation: Politics is too important to leave only to politicians. Therefore, D66 finds it important that residents are involved in important decision-making. Because people are selected by lottery to participate in citizen councils, this results in a fair and representative reflection of Emmen society.

269134.explanation: There is not enough sheltered employment available in Emmen for people with disabilities. Yet, these workplaces with guidance and adjustments are precisely what is needed to fully participate. Therefore, the municipality must prioritize ensuring that enough of these jobs become available.

269135.explanation: The municipality has been spending far too little on arts and culture for years. As a result, many institutions are struggling, and some, like Loods13, have had to close. D66 wants to change that. The new entrance to the Atlas Theater is not our highest priority in this regard.

269076.explanation: We do not want to fix the property tax (ozb) for four years in advance. Since Emmen already has a fairly high property tax, we want to keep it affordable. However, the municipality needs sufficient funds to maintain social policies. Therefore, we want to review the level of the property tax each year.

269077.explanation: We think it is important that shopkeepers can decide for themselves when they are open.

269078.explanation: Due to changing national policies or farmers voluntarily stopping, agricultural land can become available. We want to use this land as much as possible for housing and nature. This way we make our municipality greener and healthier.

269079.explanation: Many farmers are struggling. By allowing them to open farm shops, they can earn extra revenue by selling local products. And by opening campsites, there are more opportunities for recreation in our beautiful municipality.

269080.explanation: We are strong advocates of locally generated sustainable energy. The municipality should support local initiatives in every possible way. This helps keep energy bills affordable, which is especially important for people with lower incomes.

269081.explanation: Large distribution centers offer little economic benefit. They mainly employ migrant workers because working conditions are generally poor. The pressure on the surrounding area is significant, and there is insufficient housing for the migrant workers.

269082.explanation: We believe that 40% of the housing in Emmen should be social housing. Until that is the case, people in difficult situations should be prioritized for housing-for example, people with children going through a divorce, youth coming from sheltered living, and status holders who have been living in a reception center for a long time.

269083.explanation: Rapid integration into society is important not only for newcomers themselves but also for the local economy and social cohesion in the municipality.

269084.explanation: Emmen must also contribute to accommodating refugees. People in need should be helped. Currently, families and unaccompanied youth are housed in the reception center (azc). There is little to no nuisance. Therefore, we see no reason to close this reception center.

269085.explanation: We want to stimulate business activity that focuses on the future. Therefore, the municipality must prioritize the arrival of new companies rapidly transitioning away from fossil fuels. Companies polluting our environment should not receive new permits.

269086.explanation: The fireworks show was a great success. It is a beautiful new tradition. Therefore, we want the municipality to continue supporting it in the future.

269087.explanation: More and more young people suffer hearing damage due to loud music. Therefore, we support introducing stricter standards for events.

269088.explanation: Everyone in Emmen must be able to participate in society. The municipality is primarily responsible for the accessibility of public buildings. Where necessary, the municipality can support entrepreneurs in making adaptations to buildings.

269089.explanation: Sports are necessary for good health and social contacts. It must also be possible for people with lower incomes. For people up to 120% of the social assistance level, the municipality should cover these costs. And by investing in sport, we also save twice as much money on healthcare.

269091.explanation: Sports are necessary for good health and social contacts. By investing in sports, we save twice as much money on healthcare. Clubs can encounter problems for various reasons. When needed, the municipality steps in.

269092.explanation: It was already announced in January that there will be extra enforcement officers (boa's) in the municipality. There is no need for more. We prefer to invest in prevention, such as youth work.

269093.explanation: We are not fundamentally opposed to the arrival of new coffee shops. The municipality must properly supervise the owners and the location of the coffee shops to minimize nuisance. It is also important to be aware of the addictive effects and to invest in addiction care.

269094.explanation: Within the municipality, the police are the only organization authorized to use force. In case of conflicts, enforcement officers (boa's) are deployed to calm the situation.

269095.explanation: We think it is good that housing seekers from Emmen are given priority. This allows young people and starters who want to stay to continue living in our municipality. This must not come at the expense of urgent groups, such as people with children going through a divorce, youth from sheltered living, and status holders.

269096.explanation: We want even 40% social rental housing. This also applies to the more expensive areas such as Parc Sandur and Delftlanden. This way, we ensure affordable and mixed neighborhoods.

269097.explanation: Homes are for living, not for making a profit. Some neighborhoods in Emmen already have a own-occupancy obligation. We want to extend this approach to all new-build and newly purchased homes. This ensures that houses remain available to people who want to live there themselves.

269101.explanation: Social assistance benefits are a social safety net. We find it important that people are supported in finding work or other meaningful activities. Obligations can actually work against that.

269102.explanation: The municipality must continue developing basic jobs. These should primarily focus on making the municipality more sustainable and greener and caring for vulnerable residents. A basic job should not cause financial problems because benefits or allowances are lost.

269103.explanation: We stand for a solidarity society. People who need help must receive it.

269104.explanation: The municipality is not allowed to do this. National legislation (the Participation Act) prohibits it.

269106.explanation: By introducing paid parking in other major centers, parking costs in the center of Emmen can be reduced. This creates a fairer competitive position for entrepreneurs in Emmen.

269107.explanation: There are more and more electric cars. We find it important that residents of all centers can continue to charge their electric cars close to home in the future. Therefore, we consider a well-distributed network of charging points important.

269108.explanation: Cycling is a sustainable and healthy way to get to work, school, or sports club. With safe and pleasant bike paths and bike highways, we make it more attractive for residents to cycle. Moreover, cyclists should have as much priority as possible over cars.

269109.explanation: Lowering the speed limit in built-up areas to 30 kilometers per hour has many advantages. It increases safety, reduces emissions, and creates a safer environment for everyone.

269110.explanation: Village halls and neighborhood centers are important for strengthening community spirit. They play a major role in the social cohesion of a village or neighborhood.

269112.explanation: We wish our local pride a beautiful new stadium. However, FC Emmen is a commercial organization responsible for its own business operations. We support the development of the sports and exercise campus at Meerdijk.

269113.explanation: Many items brought to the recycling center can still be reused. By imposing a maximum weight of 300 kilos, the municipality encourages reuse instead of throwing away.

269114.explanation: The municipality is a shareholder of the park and has an interest in its well-being. Decisions about granting a loan should be assessed on a case-by-case basis.

269116.explanation: The Rensenpark must remain accessible to everyone. It is a unique green and recreational area in the city. This will be lost if housing construction is allowed in the park.

269117.explanation: We see the Rensenpark as the place where a thriving cultural and creative sector can be housed.

269118.explanation: Everyone has the right to a roof over their head. We support the 'Housing First' principle. This means that people are immediately provided with shelter and then guided towards recovery. With this, we want to solve homelessness. As long as this is not yet possible, we need this shelter.

269119.explanation: Many residents perceive the station area as unsafe. We advocate for a direct bus connection from Zwolle to Ter Apel. We want to continue the pilot project to have stewards at the station and support the deployment of public enforcement officers.

269120.explanation: Relocating to Emmen-South is not a solution. We prefer a direct bus connection from Zwolle to Ter Apel.

269121.explanation: The Greenwise Campus is a unique project in the Netherlands. A place where university, higher professional education, and secondary vocational education come together to shape the education of the future. By investing in this, we put Emmen on the map as a student city and build towards a green future.

269123.explanation: Market forces in healthcare need to be addressed. Together with care providers, we want to ensure that people deal with the same caregivers as much as possible and that the quality of care improves. We also support district teams that provide accessible and low-threshold help.

269124.explanation: Expropriation is a severe measure that should only be used if it serves a great public interest. The Nedersaksenlijn is very important for Emmen’s accessibility and economic development. For this reason, we do not rule out expropriation outright.

269125.explanation: We want more collective heating projects in neighborhoods, where possible in cooperation with resident initiatives such as heat communities. We must use sustainable heat sources, such as geothermal energy. For all collective heating projects, we pay attention to affordability for low-income groups.

269126.explanation: The Netherlands must be smoke-free by 2050. Ideally, we would like to see terraces become completely smoke-free now.

269127.explanation: We want to improve traffic safety. Therefore, we want a maximum speed of 30 kilometers per hour within built-up areas. Traffic calming measures like speed bumps are necessary to ensure this.

269129.explanation: Civil servants do a lot of important work for our municipality. We are proud of them. Many budget cuts have already been made on civil servants in recent years. Work is already left undone, causing project delays. Therefore, we want to invest more in the civil service apparatus.

269130.explanation: Separating waste helps people to be more conscious about natural resources.

269131.explanation: Emmen is a rainbow municipality and must live up to that name. Discrimination and intimidation of LGBTIQA+ people still occur. Therefore, we want a local Pride. Everyone should be proud and visible as themselves.

269132.explanation: We want to prioritize pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport. Where possible, we want to create bicycle streets where cars are guests.

269133.explanation: We are in favor of direct democracy. Citizens’ assemblies are a good example. They reduce polarization and increase mutual understanding.

269134.explanation: For people with a distance to the labor market, sheltered workplaces are a solution. They contribute to the well-being of vulnerable groups and provide people with meaningful daily activities.

269135.explanation: The Atlas Theater is a great success. It attracts many more visitors than expected. An entrance at the front provides better visitor flow and gives the building a better appearance.

269076.explanation: We want to lower the property tax.

269119.explanation: As long as the asylum seeker center in Ter Apel is not closed, safety in and around public transport must be guaranteed.

269120.explanation: This is not a sustainable solution to the problem of nuisance.

269076.explanation: Although property tax has remained the same for years, house prices have risen sharply, which has increased the amount of property tax to be paid.

269077.explanation: There is now a good agreement in place with the shopkeepers.

269078.explanation: Only with the consent of farmers and if it is really necessary. Agricultural land can be converted to nature or sometimes housing.

269079.explanation: This helps farmers keep their businesses future-proof, provided it fits into the environment. It is good for tourism and the continuation of farming.

269080.explanation: Local initiatives strengthen sustainability and involvement, but there are already many support options available.

269081.explanation: They take up a lot of space and provide relatively few jobs for the local area.

269082.explanation: Equal treatment in housing allocation is a basic principle.

269083.explanation: Good integration prevents problems and promotes coexistence, but money is not the solution here.

269084.explanation: Shelter must remain manageable, humane, and organized with clear boundaries. Every war victim should receive shelter.

269085.explanation: There are only a few possibilities left to establish, so in principle yes. We support sustainability, but it must remain realistic with a gradual transition rule.

269086.explanation: Yes, just like in 2025, a combination of lasers, drones, and fireworks. A safe and joint alternative to private fireworks.

269087.explanation: Hearing protection is essential for good health and livability. Young people find out too late and quickly develop problems. So yes, with an enforced end time.

269088.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate independently, and we must help remove obstacles to that.

269089.explanation: Something free has no value, so people with welfare benefits must pay 'something' for it. For severe cases, customized solutions can be provided, so exceptions can be made. This can be done through expanding the 'Join in pass' or through the Participation and Stimulation Fund. Sport is very important for health and social contacts.

269090.explanation: Attention to regional language and culture is valuable. It creates pride in where you come from and allows you to express yourself in your own dialect.

269091.explanation: Associations are indispensable for social cohesion. But there is also a responsibility on sports clubs themselves, so only if they have gotten into trouble through no fault of their own.

269092.explanation: Safety is a priority, one of our core values. On January 23rd, it was decided to appoint 6 additional public enforcement officers. In one year, we will evaluate whether this is sufficient.

269093.explanation: If more were added, it would have an attracting effect, especially on customers from Germany. It could also further promote undermining.

269094.explanation: Against, because they are there to enforce. And violence begets violence. If they are allowed anyway, then under strict conditions and with proper training.

269095.explanation: Local housing seekers must retain perspective, but currently there are too few homes available, so a fair distribution must take place.

269096.explanation: In favor: this has been approved by the council. Unfortunately, the housing corporations are unable to build new social housing.

269097.explanation: In favor, because this is already mandatory in parts of Emmen and prevents exploitative landlords.

269101.explanation: It gives those people something meaningful to do and prevents them from feeling excluded from society.

269102.explanation: Against, because many places have actually been added in the past year, solving the waiting list.

269103.explanation: Disagree. The scheme is currently good, although customization is needed.

269104.explanation: In favor, but it is up to the national government.

269106.explanation: Because that would also apply to visitors of Wildlands, and there have been large investments in parking spaces.

269107.explanation: Sufficient places have now been created, but there are no disabled charging spots. And clearly indicate where the charging spots are.

269108.explanation: Express bike lanes must be safe, so they need to be wide enough.

269109.explanation: In residential areas yes, but that is already the case now.

269110.explanation: We are very much in favor of that, because it combats loneliness and promotes "neighborliness" in villages and neighborhoods.

269112.explanation: Yes, but only for the real estate.

269113.explanation: Emmen already has too much residual waste. Besides, you can always borrow the card from a neighbor.

269114.explanation: The municipality already profits from lending money to Wildlands (municipality borrows cheaper and receives a higher interest from Wildlands). If they want to renovate, a loan is also favorable for the municipality.

269116.explanation: It was agreed that only the existing buildings may be renewed. So no new construction.

269117.explanation: The spaces are very maintenance-sensitive. Investing more money in something that is not good is wasted money.

269118.explanation: No, the night shelter was only recently moved to the new building on Weerdingerstraat.

269119.explanation: The national government must pay for this and is responsible.

269120.explanation: This way the station area is relieved. Asylum seekers arrive at Emmen-South station and go directly to Ter Apel.

269121.explanation: Currently we receive JTF subsidy. We may need to invest in facilities such as buildings and infrastructure.

269123.explanation: The current main providers are responsible for good social support (WMO) care in their area. They are also responsible for subcontractors. This is a good way for the municipality to retain control over WMO care.

269124.explanation: That will have to happen, but with a market-conform price for the land.

269125.explanation: District heating means people have no choice and become dependent on a single supplier.

269126.explanation: In favor to protect the health of residents and especially to allow people with COPD to also have a place on the terrace.

269127.explanation: At the moment there is a good balance in necessary locations to reduce speed.

269129.explanation: AI will bring other functions and some will disappear. Not yet in the coming year.

269130.explanation: That is a much more expensive solution.

269131.explanation: 50PLUS excludes no one, including LGBTI people. Everyone in Emmen with a good plan can submit an application. An honest distribution must be made based on all applications.

269132.explanation: We don’t know that yet in Emmen. Only if the pavement clearly indicates it, it can be suitable in some cases.

269133.explanation: In Emmen we have a nice EOP system from which the municipal council can get its information.

269134.explanation: The places at Menso/Emco have recently been expanded, significantly reducing the waiting list.

269135.explanation: The current entrance is reasonable and not really necessary for 50PLUS. It is also questionable how much the municipality should invest. If it can be paid from own funds, there is no problem.

269076.explanation: We want the municipality to use your money wisely. If the municipality stops wasting money on unnecessary projects, taxes for our residents don’t have to go up.

269077.explanation: Shopkeepers should be able to choose when to open their doors. This gives entrepreneurs room to grow and is good for the liveliness and economy in Emmen.

269078.explanation: Farmers are essential for our villages. We don’t want fertile agricultural land to disappear for new nature areas or solar parks; we must be careful with our agricultural space.

269079.explanation: The municipality must help farmers who want to broaden their business. We must make plans for farm shops or campings possible instead of blocking them with rules.

269080.explanation: We support citizen initiatives where residents take control themselves. The municipality facilitates by making plans easier and removing barriers. We happily help good ideas on their way without taking them over. The power lies with the residents themselves.

269081.explanation: New businesses create jobs and strengthen the economy. Therefore, we do not want to block distribution centers, provided the location is chosen very carefully. We critically assess the integration to preserve livability and the landscape.

269082.explanation: There is a great shortage of housing. We believe that our own residents and young people from the municipality of Emmen should always have priority for social rental housing.

269083.explanation: Integration means adapting to our rules and culture. We want to take strict action against nuisance; in our opinion, additional subsidies are not the solution for good integration.

269084.explanation: Residents suffer greatly from asylum seekers from the reception center and Ter Apel. The safety of our own people comes first.

269085.explanation: Sustainability regulations should not make entrepreneurship too difficult. Companies must be able to simply do their work.

269086.explanation: We support celebrations that bring people in Emmen together. This is part of our municipality's culture.

269087.explanation: We support celebrations that bring people in Emmen together. This is part of our municipality's culture, as long as the noise complies with legislation.

269088.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in our municipality, including people with disabilities. We want shops and hospitality to be accessible to everyone. By financially supporting entrepreneurs with adaptations, we make Emmen more accessible and welcoming together.

269089.explanation: Sports are healthy and bring people together. We want everyone to be able to participate, including people with low incomes. By collaborating with sports clubs and entrepreneurs, we provide a personal budget for those who need it.

269090.explanation: Drents is an important part of our culture and identity. We find it good if children are introduced to our local dialect at an early age in primary school.

269091.explanation: Sports clubs are the heart of our neighborhoods and villages. If a club faces financial difficulties, the municipality should provide support so the club can continue to exist for the residents.

269092.explanation: We want more BOA officers (public space enforcement officers) in the neighborhoods and villages. They are recognizable and ensure safety everywhere. By being more present, they can tackle nuisance more quickly.

269093.explanation: Safety and livability are paramount in our municipality. New coffeeshops often bring nuisance and drug-related problems, especially for young people. We want to prevent this by blocking new establishments and relocating troublesome coffeeshops outside the center.

269094.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to do their work safely and properly. A baton helps them better tackle crime and nuisance and ensures more respect on the streets.

269095.explanation: There is a great shortage of housing. We believe that residents from our own municipality and young people from the region should have priority when looking for social rental housing.

269096.explanation: We want enough affordable homes for everyone. By requiring at least 30% social rental homes in new construction, we ensure a place for people with lower incomes.

269097.explanation: Homes are meant to live in, not to make profit by immediately subletting them. A residence obligation ensures that starters and families have a better chance of buying a home.

269101.explanation: We believe it is fair that people on benefits give something back to society if possible. Social work also helps people stay active and find real jobs more easily.

269102.explanation: Work is the best way to participate in society. By offering subsidized jobs, we help people who have been out of work for a long time to get back to work and earn an income.

269103.explanation: We should not burden residents with very low incomes extra. Exemption from municipal taxes ensures that they can continue to make ends meet.

269104.explanation: We believe everyone can contribute, including people over 55. We want to encourage and help them find work that suits them, rather than writing them off prematurely.

269106.explanation: To keep Emmen's center attractive for visitors and entrepreneurs, parking rates must go down. This is good for local shops and the city's atmosphere.

269107.explanation: We want the municipality to make the designation of charging points as simple as possible. By quickly providing locations without complicated rules, we help residents who drive electric without unnecessary time or bureaucracy.

269108.explanation: Safe and fast bike paths are important for those who cycle to work or school. We want to improve and make the connections between villages and neighborhoods safer for everyone.

269109.explanation: We are against a general 30 km/h speed limit throughout all built-up areas. On through roads you should be able to drive smoothly, but in busy residential neighborhoods and near schools, 30 zones are very useful.

269110.explanation: A community center is where residents meet and organize activities. We want every village and neighborhood to have such a place to strengthen solidarity. We consider this a very important point.

269112.explanation: FC Emmen is the business card of our region and important for sport and the economy. The municipality must assist with a new stadium so the club and the region can continue to grow.

269113.explanation: We want to combat litter and illegal dumping. By allowing residents to bring waste to the recycling center free of charge more often, we make it easier to keep the neighborhood clean.

269114.explanation: Wildlands is an important attraction for Emmen and the region. We believe the park should be able to survive on its own strength and be self-sufficient. The municipality should no longer provide loans or guarantees. Instead, we want to help Wildlands with good accessibility, promotion, and a favorable business climate, so that they can remain successful without dependency on public funds.

269116.explanation: The Rensenpark is a green oasis for recreation and relaxation in Emmen. We want to preserve this as a public park for all residents and do not allow housing construction. We build in suitable places elsewhere to protect livability.

269117.explanation: Art and culture are important for a vibrant Emmen. However, we will not invest extra money in more workspaces in the Rensenpark. We prefer to give creatives space in existing locations so that culture is spread across all our villages and neighborhoods.

269118.explanation: The nuisance around the station must stop. We want a better, fixed place for these residents where they can find peace and the surroundings no longer suffer from them on top of the existing nuisance.

269119.explanation: Safety in and around the buses to Ter Apel is a big problem for travelers and drivers. The municipality must co-finance security to guarantee peace and safety in transport.

269120.explanation: Moving the bus stop to another station does not solve the underlying problems with nuisance. We must address the cause instead of just shifting the problem.

269121.explanation: The Greenwise Campus helps bring good education and jobs to Emmen. By investing in this, we ensure that our young people can build a good future here.

269123.explanation: By working with fewer care providers, the municipality can make better agreements about quality. We want more control and less bureaucracy so that money goes to care and not to administration.

269124.explanation: The Nedersaksenlijn provides a better connection with the rest of Northern Netherlands and Germany. This is good for our region’s accessibility and creates more jobs and opportunities.

269125.explanation: A shared heating network can help heat houses in a sustainable and affordable way. We support investments that lower the energy bills for our residents.

269126.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves if they want a smoke-free terrace. The municipality should not impose too many rules; we trust the individual choice of the hospitality entrepreneur and the customer.

269127.explanation: Safety in our streets is very important. We support speed bumps and other measures that slow down traffic so children can safely play outside in their own neighborhood.

269129.explanation: There is currently too much money going to bureaucracy. By cutting costs in the organization of the municipality and working more efficiently, we keep more money available for important matters such as safety and care.

269130.explanation: It should be easy for residents to dispose of waste. By centrally separating waste afterwards, people need fewer bins at home and more gets recycled.

269131.explanation: We support all activities that ensure everyone can participate in Emmen. However, we do not believe the municipality should provide separate subsidies for this; this can be covered by the general budgets for culture and social interaction.

269132.explanation: A bicycle street gives cyclists space and makes the road safer. We support every improvement that ensures residents can cycle safely and comfortably through our municipality.

269133.explanation: Residents know best what is happening in their neighborhood. We support initiatives where residents are directly involved in municipal plans, based on voluntariness.

269134.explanation: Everyone who wants to work must get the chance. We support expanding protected jobs so that people with disabilities can fully participate in our society.

269135.explanation: We believe community funds can be better spent at this time than on a new entrance to the theater. We should first look into whether the current situation cannot be resolved differently.

269076.explanation: Keeping the property tax (ozb) “the same” actually means an increase because the property value (WOZ) rises. That is taxation through the back door. We want to consciously set taxes, lower the ozb where possible, including for entrepreneurs, and stop automatic WOZ linkages. Taxes require political choices.

269077.explanation: Sunday openings are allowed if entrepreneurs want it themselves. The municipality facilitates but does not force. No mandatory shopping Sundays and no pressure on staff. Small shops should not be outcompeted by chains that operate seven days a week.

269078.explanation: Agricultural land is meant for food production and the local economy. Converting it to nature reduces the number of farmers and undermines rural areas. Protect agricultural land and stop one-sided nature policies that ignore practical realities and livability.

269079.explanation: Farmers are under great pressure and must be given room to earn extra income. Side activities, such as a shop or camping, strengthen the farm business and rural life. The municipality must enable this and remove obstacles.

269080.explanation: Generating energy together is fine, but it does not have to be with municipal money. The municipality should not distribute subsidies for energy projects. Initiative and risk belong to residents themselves, not the taxpayer.

269081.explanation: Distribution centers take up a lot of space, cause extra traffic, and provide few permanent jobs. They harm the landscape without real added value for Emmen. The municipality must set clear limits here.

269082.explanation: Social rental homes are scarce. Priority for status holders comes at the expense of local residents who often have been waiting for years. Housing must be fair and based on local ties, not on exception positions.

269083.explanation: Integration is important, but extra money is not the solution. Participation starts with the commitment of newcomers themselves. The municipality must set boundaries and not build an ever-growing integration apparatus with community funds.

269084.explanation: Emmen’s capacity is limited. The asylum seekers' center (azc) places long-term pressure on safety and livability. We want to work toward closure and reconsider the contract, with attention to residents and neighborhoods where the impact is greatest.

269085.explanation: The municipality should not select companies based on ideological sustainability labels. Entrepreneurship requires freedom and clear rules, not exclusion in advance. Employment, decent behavior, and compliance with legislation should be paramount.

269086.explanation: The municipality does not need to actively cooperate with or pay for a fireworks show. However, there must be room for organizers’ initiatives. Facilitate where possible, but without municipal costs, risks, or involvement beyond core tasks.

269087.explanation: Stricter noise standards are not necessary. The standards already exist but are insufficiently enforced. Residents benefit more from consistent inspection and enforcement than from ever-new rules on paper.

269088.explanation: People with disabilities must be able to participate fully. Helping entrepreneurs with practical adjustments works better than extra rules or obligations. Targeted support increases accessibility without burdening entrepreneurs with new bureaucracy.

269089.explanation: Sports are important, but full payment by the municipality goes too far. Supporting where needed is good, but personal responsibility must remain. Participation is encouraged through targeted help, not by taking over everything.

269090.explanation: Drents is part of our identity and history. Attention at school strengthens connection to the region. It is about encouragement, not obligation, and stands alongside good education in Dutch.

269091.explanation: Associations with a clear social and community function should not disappear due to temporary financial problems. The municipality may provide targeted support to preserve village life and volunteer work, without structural subsidies or blank checks.

269092.explanation: Safety starts with visibility. More enforcement officers (boa’s) in towns and villages ensure quicker intervention, less nuisance, and more trust among residents. Rules only have value if they are also enforced.

269093.explanation: More coffeeshops lead to extra nuisance, crime, and pressure on safety. Emmen already has enough challenges. The municipality must set limits and prevent further normalization of drug problems.

269094.explanation: Enforcement officers (boa’s) must be able to do their work safely. A baton is not a symbol of power but protection against threats and aggression. Those who take enforcement seriously ensure that officers can defend themselves.

269095.explanation: In case of housing shortages, it is reasonable that local residents do not consistently stand at the back of the line. Waiting time remains leading, but for new construction, limited priority within the law may apply for people with local ties to protect starters and lower incomes.

269096.explanation: Rigid percentages do not solve housing shortages. Emmen already has a high share of social rental homes. New construction must match local needs and stimulate flow, not be stuck to imposed quotas that delay building.

269097.explanation: A mandatory owner-occupancy requirement sounds appealing but hinders the free rental sector and housing flow. Speculation must be tackled with targeted measures. A general obligation makes the housing problem bigger, not smaller.

269101.explanation: Who can work, must contribute. Socially meaningful work provides structure, increases self-esteem, and prevents long-term dependency. Social assistance is a safety net, not a final destination. Tailored solutions remain necessary for those who truly cannot.

269102.explanation: Subsidized jobs do not solve structural problems. They are often temporary and keep people dependent. The focus must be on real jobs, guidance to regular work, and removing obstacles to employment.

269103.explanation: For residents with low income, debt forgiveness is often necessary to get by. Stopping this increases debts and problems. However, the scheme must remain targeted and prevent abuse.

269104.explanation: There is already room for exemptions in special circumstances. Instead of a general exemption based on age, the frameworks must be revised so that tailored solutions are actually applied, fairly and realistically.

269106.explanation: The center must remain accessible. High parking fees drive visitors away and harm local businesses. Cheaper parking enhances vibrancy and makes the center more attractive for residents and visitors.

269107.explanation: Additional charging points come at the expense of regular parking spaces and increase parking pressure. Electric driving is allowed, but public space must remain accessible to everyone. Sufficient parking space must come first before expansion.

269108.explanation: Separate fast bike paths between all centers are unrealistic and costly. Investment must be focused where traffic is heavy and unsafe. No megalomaniac plans, but practical choices with impact.

269109.explanation: A general 30 km/h rule is too coarse. Not every road is the same. Traffic safety requires tailor-made solutions: 30 km/h where necessary, 50 km/h where possible. Rules should remain logical and enforceable.

269110.explanation: Not every village or neighborhood needs its own community center. Facilities must match usage and support. Sometimes cooperation or sharing is more effective than placing a building everywhere.

269112.explanation: Financing a new stadium is not a municipal task. However, the municipality can play a facilitating role in a new business case with more own income and collaboration. Public money remains designated for core tasks, not stadium risks.

269113.explanation: 300 kilos of free waste is too little for many households. A higher limit prevents illegal dumping and encourages responsible waste disposal. That is practical, fair, and ultimately cheaper for society.

269114.explanation: The municipality must stop lending money to Wildlands. Public funds do not belong in the structural risks of a business. Operations and investments are primarily the responsibility of the park itself.

269116.explanation: Limited housing development on the edges of Rensenpark is possible, provided the park itself remains green and public. Careful construction can enhance the living environment without compromising the character of the park.

269117.explanation: Art and culture are valuable but should not be structurally dependent on subsidies. The municipality can provide space but no extra money. Art that has broad support can sustain itself.

269118.explanation: Shelters are necessary, but the current location near the station causes nuisance and insecurity. Homeless people deserve good care in a place that offers calm. That is better for them as well as for travelers and local residents.

269119.explanation: Security around the bus to Ter Apel is not a municipal task. Emmen did not ask for this shelter and is now burdened with the consequences. Costs for asylum and security belong to the national government, not our residents.

269120.explanation: Moving the bus to Emmen-Zuid shifts asylum issues to other neighborhoods and facilities. That is unacceptable. Emmen is not responsible for failing national asylum policy. Shelter, nuisance, and security should be resolved nationally, not passed down locally.

269121.explanation: Spending more money is not automatically better. First, it must be clear what it yields for jobs, entrepreneurs, and residents. Without measurable results and clear goals, extra funding is irresponsible.

269123.explanation: Fewer providers mean scaling up and dependency. That makes care vulnerable and impersonal. A variety of providers increases resilience, choice, and proximity, as long as quality and oversight are well arranged.

269124.explanation: Property is property. Expropriation for infrastructure undermines legal certainty and must not become automatic. The Nedersaksen line can only be realized with the voluntary cooperation of landowners.

269125.explanation: Collective heating systems are expensive, complex, and limit freedom of choice. Residents should not be tied to a single system. The municipality should allow room for voluntary initiatives but should not invest public money in large-scale compulsory solutions.

269126.explanation: A mandatory smoke-free terrace section is patronizing. Entrepreneurs and guests should be able to make agreements about this together. The municipality should not regulate every detail. Freedom and choice also belong in public spaces.

269127.explanation: More speed bumps are not a miracle cure. They cause noise, damage, and frustration. Traffic safety requires tailor-made solutions: enforcement, design, and speed where necessary, not the same bump everywhere.

269129.explanation: A leaner municipal organization prevents bureaucracy and speeds up decisions. Savings can be made by reducing layers of consultation and policy, not by dismantling core tasks and services.

269130.explanation: Separate waste collection is more efficient and prevents residents from being burdened with complicated rules. The waste processor can better separate waste centrally with modern techniques. That increases recycling and reduces mistakes and frustration among residents.

269131.explanation: The municipality should not grant subsidies based on identity. Everyone is equal and can participate in existing activities. Community funds are intended for general provisions, not group-targeted advocacy.

269132.explanation: More bicycle streets sound attractive but often cause confusion and false security. Traffic safety requires tailor-made measures per street. Clear rules, good design, and enforcement work better than new road labels.

269133.explanation: A citizens' assembly by lottery undermines representative democracy. Residents elect councilors to make considerations. Advice can be valuable but should come through open consultation, not through randomly selected mini-parliaments.

269134.explanation: Not everyone can keep up in the regular labor market. Sheltered workshops offer structure, dignity, and participation. These must be focused and meaningful, with possible progression to regular work.

269135.explanation: A new entrance is not a core task of the municipality. Public funds should go to care, security, and housing. The Atlas Theater functions; improvements are primarily the responsibility of the operator.

statements.title: The municipality must allow housing construction in Rensenpark (the old zoo).
The municipality must contribute to the cost of a new stadium for FC Emmen.
The asylum seekers' centre (azc) in our municipality must close as soon as possible. The contract runs until 2031.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

titleParts.text: (azc) in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten. Het contract loopt tot 2031.

statements.moreInfo: Emmen has one asylum seekers’ centre. It is located near Emmen Zuid station. This family location with space for 650 people will, according to agreements with the COA, remain open until mid-2031.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) must remain the same.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must invest more money in the Greenwise Campus on Eerste Bokslootweg.
statements.moreInfo: Greenwise Campus is a collaborative site in development. Companies, schools, government and residents work there on sustainable and innovative solutions for the future.

statements.title: Emmen and all villages must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.text: In Emmen en alle dorpen moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.moreInfo: BOAs (special enforcement officers) work for the municipality and supervise neighborhoods and villages. They watch for nuisance and minor offences and can give warnings or fines. The municipality decides how many BOAs are deployed. The police are under national direction, over which the municipality has no direct influence.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Every village and neighborhood in the municipality must have its own community center.
There must be more workplaces for people with employment disabilities (formerly called social workshops).

titleParts.glossary: People who, due to illness or a disability, have difficulty obtaining paid work.

statements.title: The municipality must build separate bike paths between all villages and neighborhoods that allow for fast cycling.
Paid parking in the center of Emmen must become cheaper.
The municipality must invest more in exhibition and work spaces for artists in Rensenpark (the old zoo).
Refugees with residence permits must be given priority for social housing.

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.moreInfo: Socially useful work is work that benefits others, such as helping in a community center, reading at school or working in green spaces. It is temporary, not excessive and must not replace paid work.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in a shared heating system for houses and other buildings.
The municipality must install more speed bumps in villages and neighborhoods.
At least 30% of the newly built homes in the municipality of Emmen must be social housing. Currently 28% of the homes in the municipality are social housing.

titleParts.text: zijn. In de gemeente is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.theme: Security for the Ter Apel bus
statements.title: The municipality must contribute to permanent security around the bus to Ter Apel.
statements.moreInfo: Asylum seekers can use a shuttle bus to the asylum seekers’ centre in Ter Apel. This bus departs from Emmen Station.

statements.title: The shelter for homeless people near Emmen station must be moved to another location.
There should be more subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Government-funded jobs for people who have little chance of getting a regular job.
People who have been without paid work for a long time (longer than a year).

statements.moreInfo: Subsidized jobs are positions where the municipality pays part of the wage. These jobs are intended for people who have been out of work for a long time or who find it difficult to get a job, so they can gain work experience.

statements.title: The municipality must work with fewer different care providers for assistance and support.
The municipality must subsidize activities for LGBTI people.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.
LHBTI stands for lesbian women (L), gay men (H), bisexuals (B), transgender people (T) and intersex persons (I).

statements.title: The amount of waste residents may bring free to the recycling center must be increased. It is currently 300 kilograms.

titleParts.text: De hoeveelheid afval die bewoners gratis naar de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

titleParts.text: mogen brengen, moet omhoog. Nu is het 300 kilo.

statements.moreInfo: Residents of the municipality of Emmen can annually hand in 300 kilograms of (coarse) household waste per household free of charge at the Area Reiniging recycling centre.

statements.title: The municipality must pay the full sports club membership for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub volledig betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must no longer lend money to the Wildlands animal park.
Emmen must have a citizens' assembly that advises the municipal council. A citizens' assembly consists of residents chosen by lot.
The Nedersaksenlijn must be built, even if the municipality has to expropriate land for it.
statements.moreInfo: The Nedersaksenlijn is a not-yet-existing rail connection from Groningen, via Emmen, to Twente. The project is intended to connect parts of the Netherlands that are currently poorly reachable by train (mainly Southeast Drenthe and Northeast Overijssel) to the main rail network. Expropriating land means the government can take land for a project that benefits everyone, such as a railway line. The owner receives payment for this.

statements.title: The municipality must create more bicycle streets where cyclists have priority over cars.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.
The municipality should encourage all primary schools to pay attention to the Drenthe dialect.
Terraces in Emmen should be required to have a smoke-free section.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.
The BOAs who monitor safety in the municipality of Emmen should be given a baton.
The municipality should allow shopkeepers in Emmen city centre to be open on Sundays more than once a month.
The municipality should reduce spending on civil servants.
The municipality should prevent agricultural land from being converted into nature areas.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should start separating household waste after it is collected.

shootoutStatements.theme: Exemption from the job application obligation
shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should exempt people over 55 on social assistance from the job-search requirement.
Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
The municipality should fund a new entrance for the Atlas Theater.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.
Noise standards at events should be made stricter.
The municipality should continue to support the fireworks show during the Derde Kerstdag event on December 27.
The municipality should ask the province to move the bus stop for the bus to the asylum seekers' registration centre in Ter Apel to Emmen-Zuid station.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.
